method_atom: A systematic enumeration-and-filtering search over a discrete space of candidate configurations generated by quotienting a higher-dimensional structure by a discrete symmetry group down to a target residual subgroup, retaining only configurations that reproduce a prescribed low-energy spectrum while preserving an exact residual discrete symmetry; unwanted extra degrees of freedom that come in conjugate pairs are eliminated by giving them mass along flat directions of a constraint surface, and the permitted couplings and mass terms are dictated by algebraic selection rules.
object_atom: Heterotic string theory compactified on a Z6-II orbifold with SO(10) and E6 local grand-unified structures, searched for vacua reproducing the minimal supersymmetric standard model spectrum with exact R parity, non-trivial Yukawa matrices for three quark and lepton families, and see-saw Majorana masses for right-handed neutrinos.
brief_reason: The symmetry-quotient construction plus enumerate/filter-for-target-spectrum-and-residual-symmetry search, with unwanted paired modes decoupling along constraint-flat directions under algebraic selection rules, is the transferable machinery, while the heterotic-orbifold string vacuum and the MSSM-with-R-parity target are the physics-specific object.
